title: O.W.L.s
prose: The Great Hall is rearranged for exams — single desks in long silent rows, the four house tables banished somewhere for the duration — and for two weeks straight your life is written parchment, practical demonstrations, and the particular ache of a hand that's been writing for three hours.
By the last exam, you can barely remember what the castle sounds like when it isn't full of quills scratching. It's a relief just to hand in the final paper and walk out into ordinary noise again.

choices.label: Try not to think about the results until they actually arrive.
